focus: Test Claude Code compatibility of top-level Copilot-safe fields on the Claude UserPromptSubmit wrapper
noveltyJustification: Only one final compatibility question remained; iteration 006 adds decisive Claude Code evidence but does not change the schema, patch, or Copilot reproducer conclusions from iterations 003-005.

graphEvents.summary: Official Claude Code docs define nested matcher wrappers with executable handlers inside the inner hooks array
Claude Code 2.1.117 accepts top-level Copilot-only type/bash/timeoutSec fields on a UserPromptSubmit wrapper
Claude executes the nested command and ignores the top-level Copilot bash field
The .github/hooks/superset-notify.json flat bash patch remains correct
The implementation-safe additional patch is to add a top-level no-op Copilot command to the Claude wrapper
Claude rejects wrapper objects with top-level Copilot-only fields
Claude executes the top-level Copilot bash field as an extra hook

summary: Official Claude Code hook docs define three nesting levels: event, matcher group, and hook handler; command hook handlers are inside the inner hooks array and use command plus timeout rather than top-level bash.
Claude Code 2.1.117 accepted a UserPromptSubmit wrapper with top-level type/bash/timeoutSec plus a nested Claude command and emitted successful hook events.
sources: shell-command-2026-04-22T14:52Z: isolated claude -p run with --settings settings-patched.json emitted hook_started and hook_response

summary: Claude Code executed only the nested Claude command and did not execute the top-level Copilot bash field.
sources: shell-command-2026-04-22T14:56Z: marker inspection showed /tmp/claude-hook-compat-iter006-1776869539/top-level-bash.log was MISSING

summary: The corrected .github/hooks/superset-notify.json patch remains the flat Copilot command shape with version, hooks, canonical event names, type, bash, and timeoutSec.
The implementation-safe additional patch is to add top-level type:"command", bash:"true", and timeoutSec:3 to the Claude UserPromptSubmit wrapper while leaving hooks[0].command unchanged.
sources: shell-command-2026-04-22T14:56Z: top-level Copilot bash marker missing while nested Claude marker existed

summary: The isolated Claude run intentionally failed after hook execution because ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL pointed at 127.0.0.1:9; the hook compatibility signal occurs before the expected API failure.
The same top-level no-op wrapper mitigation that Copilot can execute is accepted by Claude Code settings parsing and does not prevent nested hook success.
Because Claude ignores the top-level bash field, the implementation can use bash:true for Copilot without adding a duplicate Claude-side hook execution.
Claude rejects wrapper objects with top-level Copilot-only fields.
Claude executes the top-level Copilot bash field as an extra hook.
The cross-runtime-safe wrapper shape is a Claude matcher object with top-level Copilot no-op command fields plus the unchanged nested Claude command handler.
